reviews.review_description: Say hello to the love child of two of VR’s best, combining the slicing of Beat Saber with the satisfying shooting and reloading of Pistol Whip. Instead of chopping boring blocks, you’ll take down tron-esque light enemies that demand precise, directional strikes and sharpshooter accuracy. As you progress the stage, you’ll swap seamlessly between pistols and sabers, and it feels as smooth as butter once you get the hang of it. My first run felt like rubbing my belly while patting my head, but it only took minutes to adjust. The soundtrack syncs perfectly with the sleek, cyber-themed visuals for an crazy VR experience. On top of that there are cool and detailed customization options you can set up before starting.

I play this alot on pcvr and thrilled it got on the meta store. Played the demo a decent amount and its really good. I recommend this to anybody who likes rythm games. Only 1 person behind all this made the game the music everything very impressive.  Im glad I can now play this on standalone aswell

First time in 6 years of VR I nearly lost balance while transitioning levels 🙈😂😂

Fast ...frantic & not easy ..even on easy 

Not gonna compare it to the obvious but even if you've played those other games to death this is seriously gonna challenge you & love that art style 

Can't wait for the full release 

Cheers 🥂

reviews.review_title: Feel like a samurai in cyberpunk
reviews.review_description:
I've already own this game for PC but I always wanted to see how will it perform in quest and I'm quite impressed. The game looks very well on quest 3 and it takes the advantage of playing in a standalone headset.

I strongly recommend everyone to play it!

reviews.review_title: rhythm game that mixes guns and swords
reviews.review_description: It feels like playing a mix of Beat Saber and Pistol Whip! The rainy, futuristic city background, which reminds me of Blade Runner, is also very impressive. I can't wait to play the full version:)
The demo was engaging and I enjoyed the futuristic art style. Had a lot of fun slicing and shooting y way through the level. Looking forward to what the rest of the game has to offer!

Slice, dodge, shoot all in one game.
A good workout.
And the music…. Is awesome 👏 👏👏👏👏

reviews.review_title: Beat Saber or Pistol Whip? Why not both?
reviews.review_description: After quite some time, Cybrid is finally on Quest. Let's go ;)

I‘ve been playing Cybrid for some time now on steam already, and I kept waiting, hoping this game would finally make it‘s way to meta so it can be played anywhere I can take my headset.

The game is an on rails slasher/shooter that instantly immerses you in its cyberpunk noir aesthetic. You‘re running down dark streets, getting attacked from Hitmen and Cyber-Ninjas, which you can take down in style, with dual katanas or dual guns - you can even go katana+gun. Enemies are always immune to all at attacks but one.
The game‘s art style is really good at letting you laser focus on the action at hand, quickly communicating which attack will take down your opponent.
The soundtrack is on point adding an additional flavor to the cyberpunk theme and immediately making you feel the action. If timed correctly, you will slay your foes to the beat of the tunes.
Most levels have a part where time slows down and gives you the chance to take out multiple enemies before speeding back up - it feels so rewarding, like you‘re living an action movie.
Also, your are not just running in a straight line; sometimes your jumping off a bridge or down a building - and the game somehow does it‘s magic letting you experience these maneuvers without ever invoking a feeling of queasyness.
Considering that Shada Games is a one-man studio, this game is exceptional. And he even wrote the soundtrack for the game.
Everything fits so well together. The game truly deserves every single of it‘s five stars.

The game is similar to games like Beat Sabre or Pistolwhip- but it combines different mechanics to an addictive mixture. You wield swords or pistols in a stylish Cyberpunk combat setting. The visuals are really a blast and the soundtrack is cool and not annoying. At higher difficulty Levels The game is also a good workout. I personally also think that the difficulty progression through the different stages is a lot better than in Beat Sabre (where easy is much to easy and it gets Frustrating very soon).

I’ve been waiting for this to come to standalone Quest for quite some time and it’s finally here!!! It’s such a fun yet still challenging rhythm game and I’m totally into it. 10 out of 10 would recommend

I think the graphics could do with sprucing up a bit before release but the gameplay is there and it's great fun. Like another reviewer I nearly fell over in one of the transitions between stages but apart from that there was no motion sickness at all. I'll definitely be buying this when it's released.

This was fantastic on steam when it first came out , And now that it’s getting released on Quest it’s a must have 

Fantastic gameplay 
Fantastic music
Keeps you fit 

My first 5star game of 25
Not bad.. Just had to fix wrong angle for weapon vs. wrist (via menu Options) and not good weapon switch on grab button (not customizable), where you usually (want to) hold your pistol..
